methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Dronamics is a Series A stage autonomous drone company competing within the Drones & Autonomous sector. The broader peer universe consists of 8 private firms including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Rainmaker; no public comparables exist in this narrow vertical.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, as peer-median triangulation proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Drones & Autonomous cohort. The stage-progression method uses Dronamics' $43.2M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to the next financing stage, implying a $86M valuation.

The quantitative analysis supports a valuation range of $73M to $99M, with a median point of $86M. The spread reflects uncertainty inherent to single-method derivation in an illiquid, narrow peer set. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific subsector and capital environment, as well as the absence of directly comparable Series A financings within the Drones & Autonomous space to validate the step-up assumption. Additional data on near-term revenue trajectory and competitive positioning relative to the named peer set would reduce range width considerably.
